Based on your current transactions and recurring patterns, there are several high-impact opportunities to optimize how you pay for childcare, healthcare, and wellness by leveraging your benefits at Salesforce and UCHealth.
Since these strategies involve tax and payroll considerations, I'll explain the concepts educationally. Per CFP Board standards, consult a qualified tax advisor for advice specific to your situation before making decisions.
Childcare and education optimization
Your single largest variable expense is the $1,650 monthly payment to Bright Horizons Daycare, alongside occasional $220 charges for Care.com backup care. Currently, these are being paid from your checking account with after-tax dollars.
Dependent care FSA (DCFSA): Most large employers like Salesforce and UCHealth offer a DCFSA. This allows you to set aside up to $5,000 per year pre-tax. At your household income level, routing the first $5,000 of daycare costs through this account could potentially save you approximately $1,420 annually in federal and state taxes.
Backup care benefits: Many corporate partners of Bright Horizons and Care.com provide a set number of subsidized "backup care" days per year. It is worth checking your employee portal to see if those Care.com charges could be reduced to a small co-pay (often $10–$25) rather than the full $220 rate.
Health and wellness benefits
You are currently making a recurring $400 monthly contribution to HealthEquity, totaling $4,800 annually. You also have a $44 monthly Peloton subscription and occasional healthcare costs, such as your $185.40 payment to Kaiser Permanente.
Maxing out the HSA: For 2026, the family HSA contribution limit is $8,750. Since you are currently contributing $4,800, you have $3,950 in remaining tax-advantaged capacity. Increasing your monthly contribution by approximately $329 would allow you to reach this limit and maximize your triple-tax-advantaged savings.
Payroll vs. manual contributions: If your $400 HealthEquity contribution is set up as a manual transfer from your bank, you are missing out on FICA tax savings. Routing HSA contributions directly through Salesforce or UCHealth payroll bypasses the 7.65% Social Security and Medicare taxes that you cannot recover through a year-end tax deduction.
Wellness stipends: Salesforce is known for offering a wellness reimbursement (often called a lifestyle spending account). Your $44 monthly Peloton subscription likely qualifies for this, meaning you could be getting that cost fully reimbursed each month rather than paying it out of pocket.
Subscription and service tweaks
Subscription consolidation: You are paying $19.99 for Spotify Family and $15.99 for Disney Plus. While these are relatively small, check if your cell phone provider or any of your premium credit cards offer "digital entertainment" credits that could cover these costs.
Insurance review: You are paying State Farm $218 monthly for auto insurance and $154 for home insurance ($372 combined). Large employers often have partnerships with insurers like MetLife or Liberty Mutual that offer group-rate discounts specifically for employees. A quick quote through your benefits portal could potentially lower these fixed costs.
Would you like me to help you calculate exactly how much your take-home pay would change if you increased your HSA contributions to the 2026 limit? 📈
